pilot_onboarding.text: Before we get started on the actual cases, I need you to review your employment paperwork. I've sent over the Contract and our Internal Operating Policy. Give them a thorough read and let me know when you're ready to sign.

pilot_review_loop.text: Need more time? Or are you ready to confirm everything is in order?

options.text: I've read them. Everything looks standard, I'm ready to sign.
Wait, I found a discrepancy. The IP clauses in the contract and the policy don't match.

pilot_warn_1.text: Standard? You sure about that? Usually people have questions about the fine print. You might want to look closer at the legal jargon.

options.text: I'm sure. I've seen these types of contracts before.
Actually, let me double-check the sections again.

pilot_warn_2.text: I'd really recommend comparing the Intellectual Property clause in the Contract with the one in the Policy. They seem... inconsistent regarding your personal work.

options.text: It's fine. I'm not worried about side-projects.
You're right, let me re-read those specific parts.

pilot_fired.text: Listen carefully. If you can't catch a blatant contradiction in your own hiring papers, I can't trust you to catch mistakes in a murder investigation. Look at the Contract vs the Policy. If you think this is 'fine', then you're a liability to this department. We're done here.

pilot_success.text: Good catch. It was a test. I need to know you're actually looking at the data, not just clicking through. Marcus was right, you've got the eyes for this. Welcome to the team.

options.text: Thank you, Detective. Ready for the first case.
